cover.title: 8 lines manipulators say to control you:
cover.subtitle: (and the calm reply that ends it)

slides.heading: 1. "You're overreacting."
slides.body:
Translation:
"Please doubt yourself
so I don't have to answer."
Calm reply:
"Maybe. Let's talk about
what you actually did."

slides.heading: 2. "After everything I've done for you."
slides.body:
This isn't love.
It's an invoice.
Kindness with a balance owed
was never kindness.
Calm reply:
"I'm grateful. I'm still saying no."

slides.heading: 3. "I never said that."
slides.body:
They rewrite the room
until you stop trusting
your own memory.
Calm reply:
"I remember it clearly.
We don't have to agree on it."

slides.heading: 4. "Everyone agrees with me."
slides.body:
An invisible crowd
you can never question.
That's not consensus.
That's pressure.
Calm reply:
"Then let's stick to
what you think."

slides.heading: 5. "If you really loved me, you would."
slides.body:
Love is being weaponized
to skip your consent.
Calm reply:
"I love you.
That's exactly why
I'm not doing this."

slides.heading: 6. "Calm down — it's not a big deal."
slides.body:
They shrink your reaction
so they never face the cause.
Calm reply:
"It's a big deal to me.
That's enough reason
to talk about it."

slides.heading: 7. "I guess I'm just a terrible person."
slides.body:
Now you're comforting them
for the thing they did to you.
The blame just changed seats.
Calm reply:
"This isn't about labels.
It's about what happens next."

slides.heading: 8. The silent treatment.
slides.body:
Withholding a reply
until you chase, apologize, fold.
Silence used as a leash
is still control.
Calm reply:
Don't chase.
Let the quiet cost them, not you.

slides.heading: The shift.
slides.body:
Manipulation needs one thing
to work: your reaction.
The calmer you get,
the less they can move you.
You don't win by arguing louder.
You win by staying clear.
Save this for the next time
someone tries it.
